name: Labeled Synthetic Responses Are Framed as Pre-pilot Scorer Checks, Not Replacement Validation
statement: Frohn et al. explicitly place labeled synthetic student responses in a pre-pilot validation role for LLM constructed-response scoring, using human labels and agreement metrics to revise items and scoring criteria before real student pilot validation.
population_or_context: Commercial LLM scoring of constructed-response assessment items.
time_horizon: Conference paper full text retrieved on 2026-06-02.
quantitative_note: The framework discusses agreement thresholds such as quadratic weighted kappa against human labels, along with accuracy, F1, Matthews correlation coefficient, false-positive rates, and false-negative rates.
caveats: The source is a framework paper rather than a single empirical scorer-validation study.
The paper treats real student responses as necessary for pilot validation after synthetic pre-pilot checks.
Synthetic-response generation details are not fixed to one published empirical protocol.
evaluation_method: Human-labeled synthetic responses and challenge sets are used to evaluate LLM scorer performance before pilot testing on real student responses.
reported_metrics: Cohen kappa, quadratic weighted kappa, accuracy, F1, Matthews correlation coefficient, false-positive and false-negative rates, score distributions, and conditional accuracy.
limitations: Does not establish that synthetic responses can replace authentic pilot data.
Specific thresholds and sample sizes must be instantiated for each assessment context.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The finding paraphrases the pre-pilot validation section describing labeled synthetic student responses, agreement metrics, and later real-response pilot testing.
